channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump-backed House candidate rips Biden's 'joke' border policy for inviting gang violence to swing state
item.description: Fox News Digital spoke to AZ GOP congressional candidate Abe Hamadeh and GOP Chairwoman Gina Swoboda who warned that transnational gangs are coming across the border.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What presidential campaign? The Electoral College puts most American voters on the sidelines
item.description: WAUKEGAN, Ill. (AP) — On a table at the office of the Waukegan Township Democrats sits a box of postcards with Wisconsin addresses that were collected during a postcard-writing pizza party to help turn out voters there. Leaning against the table are homemade Harris-Walz signs.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Election drop boxes open in San Diego County
item.description: While mail ballots are expected to arrive this week for registered voters, 150 official ballot drop boxes opened Tuesday in San Diego County for the November Presidential General Election.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: Fight to control NJ child care costs continues
item.description: HACKENSACK, N.J. (PIX11) - Elizabeth Velazquez is a mother to three, two of whom go to Little Learners Child Development Center in Hackensack. "My children mean the world to me," she said, holding back tears, "and I want to be able to continue to work hard every day to provide the world to them.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Harris gets personal in media blitz, balks at breaking with Biden on policy
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Kamala Harris wanted to help voters get to know her better with a cascade of media appearances Tuesday, but the most lasting impression might have been her unwillingness to break with Joe Biden.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Candidates for Las Vegas Mayor face off in a debate ahead of Election Day
item.description: Shelley Berkley will face off against Victoria Seaman in the non-partisan race. That debate will also be hosted by Denise Valdez, John Langeler, and Joshua Peguero.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, NewsNation

item.title: Fox News Politics: Harris on '60 minutes': Joe 2.0?
item.description: The latest updates from the 2024 campaign trail, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court signals support for Biden administration regulation of 'ghost gun' kits
item.description: Supreme Court signals support for Biden administration regulation of "ghost gun" ready-to-assemble kits in Garland v. VanDerStok (23-852). A ruling is expected by summer 2025.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: With no government shutdown, it feels like Christmas in September
item.description: This spending bill funds the government through October, November and just before Christmas, setting up another showdown just before Christmas Eve.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cook Political Report shifts Wisconsin Senate race toward GOP
item.description: The Cook Political Report on Tuesday shifted the Wisconsin Senate race from "lean Democrat" to "toss up," handing Republican Eric Hovde a boost as he tries to unseat Sen. Tammy Baldwin (D-Wis.).
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Dolton Mayor Tiffany Henyard walks out as trustees address budget deficit
item.description: "I just want to come here and lay the law down as it relates to what's going on in our village, where we are, and where we're going," Dolton Mayor Tiffany Henyard said.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court leans toward upholding Biden's 'ghost gun' crackdown
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday appeared to lean toward upholding the Biden administration’s regulations of so-called ghost guns. Sold as do-it-yourself kits, the devices enable a user to assemble a fully functional firearm in a matter of minutes.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden cancels overseas trip as Milton bears down on Florida; DeSantis tells VP 'it's not about you Kamala'
item.description: President Biden cancels an international trip to oversee federal storm preparations and response efforts, while Vice President Kamala Harris and Gov. Ron DeSantis trade fire as Hurricane Milton nears Florida.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How is Mayor Adams connected to the latest federal indictment?
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- In charging Mohamed Bahi with witness tampering and destroying records, federal authorities have shed light on the separate case against Mayor Eric Adams.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: DeSantis pushes back on 'delusional' Harris remark about Hurricane Milton calls
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis (R) said Monday that it was “delusional” for Vice President Kamala Harris to accuse him of “political gamesmanship” after reports emerged that he did not take the Democratic presidential nominee’s calls as his state braces for Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Harris pressed on changing positions, economic plan in '60 Minutes' interview
item.description: Vice President Harris was pressed on her changing policy positions and her expensive economic plan on “60 Minutes,” in what proved to be her toughest media interview since becoming the Democratic nominee.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Your guide to voting in Minnesota: Registration, absentee ballots and general election details
item.description: We are less than a month out from Election Day. Here are answers to some frequently asked questions MPR News readers asked us about the upcoming election.
item.dc:creator: Anika Besst and Nicole Johnson, MPR News

item.title: Harris and Trump mark somber anniversary of Hamas’ Oct. 7 attack on Israel
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former President Donald Trump and Vice President Kamala Harris marked the anniversary of the deadliest attack on Jews since the Holocaust as the presidential candidates approach the final weeks of the campaign during a widening conflict in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, COLLEEN LONG and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Presidents position selves as 'savior' to acquire power: O'Reilly
item.description: O'Reilly argues that Trump is seen as a savior by his base due to his direct approach to their fears about cultural and national changes.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: NJ senior property tax relief bill clears hurdle
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) - A bill in New Jersey tied to a program designed to cut seniors' property taxes in half cleared another hurdle in Trenton. If it's implemented, StayNJ would give a property tax break of up to $6,500 to seniors 65 and older making under half a million dollars a year.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Michael Naft discusses traffic safety, F1, and animal welfare in the Las Vegas valley
item.description: Michael Naft, incumbent county commissioner for District 8, joined Brian Loftis on the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide Monday, discussing his campaign priorities as election day nears.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Exclusive poll shows tight races for NY-17 and NY-18 congressional seats
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- New exclusive PIX11 polling with Emerson College and The Hill reveals two incredibly close races and even a ballot controversy just four weeks from election day.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Pro-Trump Christian nationalists are on tour to recruit election workers
item.description: “Are you guys ready to show the world that Christians will be silent no more?” said one speaker, whose nonprofit is recruiting people to become election workers.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Hagen and Audrey Nguyen, MPR News

item.title: Cruz posts $21 million fundraising haul in battle with Allred
item.description: Sen. Ted Cruz (R-Texas) raked in $21 million in the third fundraising quarter of 2024, he announced Monday. Cruz’s team said in a statement that the total includes money raised by his reelection campaign, political action committee and joint fundraising committee.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Springfield, Ohio, not the only small town facing immigrant influxes
item.description: Several rural and small towns say initial challenges with immigrants is a small price to pay for future prosperity.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Can you stop calls and texts from political campaigns?
item.description: If you've received a political text or call recently, you may be wondering how to make it stop, or what you can do to prevent them in the future.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Jarpe, NewsNation

item.title: Harris, Walz to do several high-profile interviews this week
item.description: Harris' media stops this week will include 60 minutes, Howard Stern The View, the Call Her Daddy podcast and Univision.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump is returning to Butler, the site of the 1st assassination attempt against him
item.description: The rally will mark Trump's first time back in the Pennsylvania city since the failed assassination attempt against him in July. The guest list will include a shooting survivor, as well as Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben, MPR News

item.title: Biden says small business disaster loan program will soon run out of money
item.description: President Joe Biden says Congress needs to shore up a small business disaster loans fund, and fast. Meanwhile, the response to Hurricane Helene has become a presidential election issue.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith, MPR News

item.title: Craig, Teirab tangle over Social Security, abortion in first head-to-head 2nd District debate
item.description: In a policy driven debate, candidates in Minnesota’s 2nd Congressional District part on fiscal and abortion policy while finding common ground on response to Jan. 6 Capitol uprising.
item.dc:creator: Ellie Roth and Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Politics Friday: Minnesota’s 2nd Congressional District debate
item.description: Minnesota’s 2nd Congressional District is one of the most closely watched races in the nation. MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst moderated a debate between Democratic Rep. Angie Craig and Republican candidate Joe Teirab on Friday, Oct. 4.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, Matthew Alvarez, and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Obama to campaign for Harris in key swing states, starting with Pennsylvania
item.description: Former President Obama is set to hit the campaign trail for Vice President Harris this month, starting his blitz in the key battleground state of Pennsylvania next week.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Minneapolis mayor, City Council clash on homeless shelter funding
item.description: The Minneapolis City Council is launching an inquiry into the city administration’s financial reporting practices following a dispute over funding for a homeless shelter.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Hurricane Helene scrambles politics in 3 battleground states
item.description: The 500-mile-long path of destruction cut by Hurricane Helene has scrambled the politics of three battleground states that could determine control of the White House and Senate: North Carolina, Georgia and Florida.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: ACLU of Nevada attempts to stop purge of nearly 20,000 registered voters
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- The ACLU of Nevada intervened in a court action Wednesday, which attempts to stop the purging of nearly 20,000 registered voters weeks before the presidential election. On Sept.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Poll: Who won the Walz-Vance VP Debate?
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Political scientists will tell you that vice presidential nominees rarely swing the election in favor of the candidate at the top of the ticket. But poll after poll shows this election cycle coming down to a percentage point across a handful of key swing states.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, Fox 5
